title: Ensure that a limit is set on pod PIDs

input_row.title: Ensure that a limit is set on pod PIDs
input_row.description: Ensure that the Kubelet sets limits on the number of PIDs that can be created by pods running on the node.
input_row.rationale: By default pods running in a cluster can consume any number of PIDs, potentially exhausting the resources available on the node. Setting an appropriate limit reduces the risk of a denial of service attack on cluster nodes. Impact: Setting this value will restrict the number of processes per pod. If this limit is lower than the number of PIDs required by a pod it will not operate.
input_row.audit: Review the Kubelet's start-up parameters for the value of --pod-max-pids, and check the Kubelet configuration file for the PodPidsLimit . If neither of these values is set, then there is no limit in place.
input_row.remediation: Decide on an appropriate level for this parameter and set it, either via the --pod-max- pids command line parameter or the PodPidsLimit configuration file setting. Default Value: By default the number of PIDs is not limited. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/pid-limiting/#pod-pid-limits
input_row.impact: Setting this value will restrict the number of processes per pod. If this limit is lower than the number of PIDs required by a pod it will not operate.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kubelet_pod_pids_limit_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the Kubelet configuration on each worker node to verify that a limit is set on the number of PIDs that can be created by pods.
- Specifically, check for the presence and value of the `--pod-max-pids` command-line flag or the `podPidsLimit` field in the Kubelet configuration file.
- Methods:
  - If using the Kubelet config file (typically referenced via `--config` flag), retrieve and inspect the file (commonly `/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml`) for the `podPidsLimit` field.
  - If using command-line flags, check the Kubelet process arguments for `--pod-max-pids`.
- Example automated checks:
  - For Kubelet config file:
    - Use SSH or DaemonSet to access `/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml` on each node and parse for `podPidsLimit`.
    - Pass if `podPidsLimit` is set to a positive integer; fail if missing or zero.
  - For command-line flags:
    - Use `ps` or equivalent via DaemonSet to inspect Kubelet process arguments for `--pod-max-pids`.
    - Pass if `--pod-max-pids` is set to a positive integer; fail if missing or zero.
- Pass/Fail Criteria:
  - **Pass:** At least one of `podPidsLimit` (in config file) or `--pod-max-pids` (in process args) is set to a positive integer on every worker node.
  - **Fail:** Neither is set, or value is zero/unlimited, on any node.
- Kubernetes API does not directly expose Kubelet config, so automation typically requires a privileged DaemonSet or node-level agent to collect and report these settings cluster-wide.
